VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I sampled a number of the rounds and, for every value checked, the arithmetic was consistent.  
• Round 0: step2 (L0073) + K[0] (L0068) = step3 (L0074); step3 + W[0] = temp1 (L0075). The hexadecimal addition 0xB0EDBDD0 + 0x428A2F98 = 0xF377ED68, exactly the bit pattern shown in L0074.  
• Round 1: h (L0083) + S1 (L0084) = step1 (L0086); step1 + ch (L0085) = step2 (L0087). 0x1F83D9AB + 0x15FBF2E4 = 0x357FCC8F, which matches the bits in L0086.  
• Round 42 (a mid-trace check): h (L0657) + S1 (L0658) = step1 (L0660); confirming in hex 0x6F2705D5 + 0x00F0C89D = 0x7017CE72, the exact pattern in L0660.  
• Round 52: step3 (L0802) + W[52] (L0796) = temp1 (L0803). 0x25CB54A1 + 0x96C9FA57 = 0xBC954EF8, identical to the bits at L0803.  
• Round 56: h + S1 (L0856) and the subsequent add with ch (L0857) give 0x32988176, precisely the step1 bits shown.  
Since every spot-check obeyed the SHA-256 arithmetic and no contradictory sum or rotation appeared, there is no evidence of a single-bit tamper.
